name: title
value: Barriers to sexual and reproductive health care faced by transgender and gender diverse people: a systematic review

name: authors
value: ["Connor Luke Allen", "Luke Muschialli", "\u00c5sa Nihl\u00e9n", "Anna Coates", "Lianne M. Gonsalves"]

name: main_contribution
value: The paper systematically identifies and characterises barriers faced by transgender and gender diverse people when accessing sexual and reproductive health services across multiple domains, including fertility care, abortion care, contraception, reproductive cancer screening, and antenatal, intrapartum and postnatal care.

name: keywords
value: ["Transgender", "Gender diverse", "Gender-inclusive care", "Sexual and reproductive health", "Barriers", "Systematic review", "Fertility care", "Abortion care", "Contraception", "Reproductive cancer screening", "Antenatal care", "Intrapartum care", "Postnatal care"]

name: abstract_summary
value: This systematic review examines evidence on barriers to sexual and reproductive health services faced by transgender and gender diverse people. The authors searched multiple databases for qualitative, quantitative and mixed-methods studies published up to August 17, 2023, and included 53 eligible studies. The review found barriers across fertility care, abortion care, contraception, reproductive cancer screening, and antenatal, intrapartum and postnatal care. Key barriers included financial inaccessibility, lack of provider knowledge and training, limited availability of relevant information, systemic and interpersonal discrimination, cisnormative and gender-biased health system practices, and dysphoria associated with procedures or treatments. The review concludes that trans and gender diverse people face both shared and unique barriers to sexual and reproductive health care, requiring system-level and individual-level reforms to make services gender-responsive and gender-inclusive.
